title: Delicious Greek Snapper Stifado
description: A sweet, chewy, cozy Greek stew featuring red snapper with double-fried crispness and a buttery tomato sauce.
instructions: Pat snapper fillets dry and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large copper pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add fillets and fry 5 minutes on one side until golden, then flip and cook another 4 minutes, remove to a plate.
Increase heat to high, add remaining 1 tbsp oil, and when it reaches 190C, fry fillets again 1 minute per side until crisp.
Remove fillets again and set aside.
In the same pot, add chopped onion and minced garlic, sauté until translucent.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to caramelize.
Add cherry tomatoes, red wine, and red wine vinegar, cook until tomatoes soften.
Pour in vegetable broth and honey, bring to a simmer.
Return fillets to the pot, cover, and let stew cook 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Sprinkle dried oregano, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley, from the copper pot.
